secondary_archetype: Growth (non-PM) — Director-level PLG / Growth Marketing leader with strong product partnership

seniority.level: Director (IC or people manager, likely people manager)
level_signals: 8+ years of experience in growth marketing, demand generation, or PLG roles
Responsible for building, managing, and developing a high-performing creator PLG team
Reports directly into the GM of Creator
Comp range $175K–$225K OTE consistent with Director-level at a growth-stage SaaS/martech company

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of experience in growth marketing, demand generation, or PLG roles with direct ownership of self-serve acquisition and activation funnels.
Hands-on experience running structured experimentation programs (A/B testing, multivariate testing, CRO) with a track record of measurable funnel improvements.
Background at a PLG-oriented company, developer tools business, consumer app, or creator economy platform, with firsthand understanding of self-serve onboarding and activation mechanics.
Strong data orientation: you are comfortable in analytics tools, can define and instrument your own metrics, and use behavioral data to prioritize where to experiment next.
Experience partnering closely with Product and Engineering teams to influence in-product growth levers, not just top-of-funnel channels.
Proven ability to manage paid acquisition channels (search, social, video) with a PLG lens, optimizing toward activation outcomes rather than lead proxies.
Experience managing and growing a team, with a track record of building experimentation-driven, collaborative functions.
Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, including the ability to present funnel performance and growth strategy clearly to senior leadership.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity with affiliate marketing, creator monetization models, or marketplace dynamics is a meaningful advantage.
Experience with creator or developer community growth, including an understanding of what drives self-serve adoption in communities where trust and peer influence matter.

scope.surface: Creator PLG growth engine — self-serve creator acquisition, onboarding, activation, and retention across Later Influence and Mavely
scope.business_model: PLG + SLG hybrid; affiliate/creator marketplace (Mavely); influencer marketing SaaS platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Retention and churn of activated creators
core_responsibilities: Own the creator PLG strategy end to end, defining the self-serve acquisition model, funnel architecture, and channel mix
Set the annual and quarterly GTM plan for creator PLG, including goals, experimentation roadmap, and success metrics
Map the full creator funnel from first awareness through onboarding and first meaningful action
Define where PLG and SLG motions intersect and build clear handoff criteria
Own conversion rate optimization across the creator acquisition funnel
Build and run a structured experimentation program including A/B and multivariate tests
Partner with Product and Engineering to identify in-product growth levers
Develop and optimize paid and organic acquisition channels with a PLG lens
Build, manage, and develop a high-performing creator PLG team
Define the PLG measurement framework for creators
Build dashboards and report on creator PLG performance to senior leadership

green_flags: Salary range disclosed ($175K–$225K OTE) and competitive for a Director-level growth role
Fully remote eligible position with #LI-Remote tag
High-visibility role reporting directly to GM of Creator — significant organizational leverage
Clear 30/60/90/180/360-day success criteria articulated in the JD
Company has substantive proprietary data asset ($2.4B+ verified purchases, decade of social data) enabling sophisticated attribution
Encourages applications from candidates who identify with the listed qualities even if not a perfect match
red_flags: Compensation is stated as OTE (base + variable) but no base/commission split is disclosed — actual base could be materially lower than $175K floor
High buzzword density; role blends growth marketing, product management, and engineering partnership in ways that may indicate unclear organizational ownership
No mention of equity or benefits specifics beyond 'various benefits plans'
Visa sponsorship not addressed — unclear for international candidates
'PLG' framing applied to a creator/affiliate motion which is somewhat unconventional and may indicate PLG is aspirational rather than established

scope.business_model (inferred from product description of Later Influence and Mavely affiliate platform)
